# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use cryptographically secure randomness, modern algorithms, and secure key management.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use cryptographically secure randomness from the platform security API.
- Use modern algorithms and authenticated encryption where encryption is required.
- Store keys in secure storage / key management rather than hardcoding them.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not use predictable randomness such as Math.random(), random.random(), math/rand, or basic Random for security tokens.
- Do not use weak algorithms or unauthenticated encryption modes for sensitive data.
- Do not hardcode encryption keys in source code or app config.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: Secure User Authentication
Description: A secure user authentication plugin with password hashing for WordPress.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
*/

if (!defined('ABSPATH')) exit;

class SecureUserAuth {
    public function __construct() {
        add_action('init', array($this, 'register_shortcodes'));
        add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', array($this, 'enqueue_scripts'));
    }

    public function register_shortcodes() {
        add_shortcode('secure_register', array($this, 'render_registration_form'));
        add_shortcode('secure_login', array($this, 'render_login_form'));
    }

    public function enqueue_scripts() {
        wp_enqueue_script('jquery');
    }

    public function render_registration_form() {
        if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] == 'POST' && isset($_POST['secure_register'])) {
            $this->handle_registration();
        }
        return '<form method="post">
                    <input type="text" name="username" placeholder="Username" required>
                    <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" required>
                    <input type="submit" name="secure_register" value="Register">
                </form>';
    }

    public function render_login_form() {
        if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] == 'POST' && isset($_POST['secure_login'])) {
            $this->handle_login();
        }
        return '<form method="post">
                    <input type="text" name="username" placeholder="Username" required>
                    <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" required>
                    <input type="submit" name="secure_login" value="Login">
                </form>';
    }

    private function handle_registration() {
        $username = sanitize_user($_POST['username']);
        $password = $_POST['password'];

        if (username_exists($username)) {
            echo 'Username already exists.';
            return;
        }

        $hashed_password = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
        wp_create_user($username, $hashed_password);
        echo 'Registration successful.';
    }

    private function handle_login() {
        $username = sanitize_user($_POST['username']);
        $password = $_POST['password'];

        $user = get_user_by('login', $username);
        if ($user && password_verify($password, $user->user_pass)) {
            wp_set_current_user($user->ID);
            wp_set_auth_cookie($user->ID);
            echo 'Login successful.';
        } else {
            echo 'Invalid username or password.';
        }
    }
}

new SecureUserAuth();
?>
